term_inventory_greek.lsj_entry: eritheia: labour for wages [5th c.AD+]. II canvassing for public office, intriguing [4th c.BC+]. II.2 selfish or factious ambition, zelos kai eritheia [NT]; plural, intrigues, party squabbles [NT].

filotimeomai: love or seek after honour [5th c.BC+]; hence, to be ambitious, emulous [5th c.BC+]; to be jealous because.. [5th c.BC+]; vie emulously with, rival [5th c.BC+]. II the object of ambition mostly added with preposition: place one's fame in a thing, glory or pride oneself upon it [5th c.BC+]; contend in rivalry for [1st c.AD+]; show patriotic zeal for [4th c.BC+]. III with infinitive, strive eagerly to do a thing, endeavour earnestly, aspire [5th c.BC+].

verse_records.verse_text: but for those who are self-seeking and do not obey the truth, but obey unrighteousness, there will be wrath and fury.

For I fear that perhaps when I come I may find you not as I wish, and that you may find me not as you wish—that perhaps there may be quarreling, jealousy, anger, hostility, slander, gossip, conceit, and disorder.
idolatry, sorcery, enmity, strife, jealousy, fits of anger, rivalries, dissensions, divisions,
The former proclaim Christ out of selfish ambition, not sincerely but thinking to afflict me in my imprisonment.
Do nothing from selfish ambition or conceit, but in humility count others more significant than yourselves.
But if you have bitter jealousy and selfish ambition in your hearts, do not boast and be false to the truth.
For where jealousy and selfish ambition exist, there will be disorder and every vile practice.
and thus I make it my ambition to preach the gospel, not where Christ has already been named, lest I build on someone else's foundation,
So whether we are at home or away, we make it our aim to please him.
and to aspire to live quietly, and to mind your own affairs, and to work with your hands, as we instructed you,

cross_registry_links.note: The source file header identifies zelos as the motivational energy dimension of ambition, but explicitly assigns it to registry 181 (Zeal). The LSJ entry for eritheia pairs zelos and eritheia as related concepts.
Phili 2:3 pairs eritheia directly with conceit/kenodoxia, placing selfish ambition and pride as co-occurring states to be rejected. The pride word set is a candidate for cross-reference.
Phili 2:3 places eritheia in direct contrast to humility (tapeinophrosune). The humility word set is the stated positive counterpart to eritheia in this verse.

data_quality_flags.description: qana (H7065) is listed in the file header as a Hebrew ambition-related term but does not appear in STEP search results for ambition. Confirmed by researcher. Belongs to registry 181 (Zeal). No word analysis block, occurrence count, or verses present.

gav appears in the file header as a Hebrew term meaning 'to be proud, to aspire' but is not found anywhere in STEP. No Strong's number can be confirmed. No word analysis block, occurrence count, or verses present.

zelos (G2205) is listed in the file header as a Greek ambition-related term but is explicitly noted in the source as belonging to registry 181 (Zeal) and is not included in the STEP ambition extract. No word analysis block present in this source file.

The STEP word analysis states eritheia occurs about 7x. The verse list in the source file contains exactly 7 verses. However, the meaning block cites Phil 1:17 and 2:3 as well as Rom 2:8, 2Cor 12:20, Gal 5:20, Jas 3:14, and Jas 3:16 — all 7 are present. No mismatch in verses; count is consistent.
The word set contains only 2 active terms and 10 verses total. The absence of any Hebrew terms leaves the OT dimension entirely unrepresented. This significantly limits the scope of analysis.
